title: chytraculia selloana
intro: Chytraculia selloana, also known as the tree marigold or devil's claw, is a perennial plant native to South Africa that belongs to the family Pedaliaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds. The health benefits of Chytraculia selloana include relief from arthritis, back pain, and other musculoskeletal issues, as well as its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots are often used to make teas, capsules, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
